scenarios.label: CO2 1500/415, OA min, symptoms, drain-pan water — no causation verdict
scenarios.prompt: 3rd-floor open office. CO2 1500 ppm, outdoor 415 ppm, HVAC OA damper at minimum, occupants reporting symptoms, standing water in the drain pan. What does this tell us?
scenarios.answer:
## Assessment context
- 3rd-floor open office; indoor CO₂ 1500 ppm against 415 ppm outdoor (≈1085 ppm differential); OA damper at minimum; occupant symptoms reported; standing water in the HVAC drain pan.
- Missing: outdoor-air CFM, occupancy count/denominator, HVAC runtime/operating mode, moisture reading on materials near the drain pan.

## Screening interpretation
- The ~1085 ppm indoor–outdoor CO₂ differential is a ventilation-adequacy indicator, not a toxic exposure; per ASHRAE 62.1 it points to outdoor-air delivery lagging occupancy with the damper at minimum.
- Standing water in the drain pan is a moisture source worth documenting; on its own it does not establish a reason for the reported symptoms.

## Recommended next steps
1. Measure delivered outdoor-air CFM and compare it against the ASHRAE 62.1 breathing-zone rate for the occupancy.
2. Capture occupancy count and HVAC operating mode at the time of the CO₂ reading to anchor the differential.
3. Document the drain-pan moisture (photo plus a moisture reading on adjacent materials) and route it to maintenance.

## Defensibility note
CO₂ here is screening evidence of ventilation adequacy only; symptom causation and any moisture or microbial determination need more data and a qualified industrial hygienist.

IH Review Required

scenarios.label: CO2 1700 / TVOC 900 rising, renovation + chemicals — TVOC screening, tool-backed, unit-correct
scenarios.prompt: Same zone now: CO2 1700 (was 1180), TVOC 900 µg/m³ (was 680), recent renovation, cleaning chemicals on site. Interpret.
scenarios.answer:
## Assessment context
- Same 3rd-floor zone; CO₂ 1700 ppm (up from 1180) and TVOC 900 µg/m³ (up from 680); recent renovation; cleaning chemicals staged on-site.
- Missing: VOC speciation, SDS for the staged products, renovation material list, ventilation runtime since the renovation.

## Screening interpretation
- Rising CO₂ and TVOC track together, in line with reduced outdoor-air dilution after the renovation; TVOC at 900 µg/m³ is a screening number from a non-speciating PID, not a health threshold.
- Against the 1991 TVOC dose-response tiers (search_standards_corpus), 900 µg/m³ falls in the 200–3000 µg/m³ multifactorial band — symptoms possible alongside other factors, not a conclusion on its own. It also exceeds the general/LEED 500 µg/m³ building-acceptance target, which is a reason to speciate before reading anything into it.

## Recommended next steps
1. Pull the SDS for every staged cleaning product and renovation material to identify the candidate VOC constituents.
2. Speciate the VOCs via EPA TO-15 (Summa canister) or TO-17 (sorbent tube), then evaluate each compound against its own PEL/TLV/REL.
3. Re-measure CO₂ and TVOC after restoring outdoor-air delivery to separate the ventilation signal from any source signal.

## Defensibility note
TVOC is a non-speciated screening number; any health interpretation needs speciation and a qualified industrial hygienist. No causal or compliance call is made here.

IH Review Required
